questions.question: Define parse tree and explain its components.
keyPoints: Parse tree is a hierarchical representation of syntactic structure

questions.question: Explain the difference between leftmost and rightmost derivations with example.
keyPoints: Leftmost: leftmost non-terminal replaced at each step
Rightmost: rightmost non-terminal replaced at each step
Both can produce same parse tree

questions.question: What is an ambiguous grammar? How can ambiguity be resolved?
keyPoints: Ambiguous: multiple parse trees for same string
Methods: rewrite grammar, use disambiguation rules
Inherent ambiguity cannot be resolved by grammar modification

questions.question: Explain preorder, inorder, and postorder traversals of parse trees with output notation.
Differentiate between parse tree and Abstract Syntax Tree (AST).
keyPoints: Parse tree: concrete syntax, includes all details
AST: abstract syntax, removes redundant info
Parse tree shows all production rules used
AST is closer to meaning, parse tree to syntax

questions.question: Define: (i) Yield (ii) Sentence (iii) Sentential form
keyPoints: Yield: string of terminals at leaves
Sentential form: mix of terminals and non-terminals

questions.question: Explain how parse tree is constructed for an arithmetic expression.
